narration: You upgrade your phone every 22 months. Most people do it while the old one still works fine. But nobody's shown you the real price tag.
visual: Smartphone SVG centered on dark background with '$1,000' price tag on screen, dollar signs animated floating upward away from the phone, '22 MONTHS' in large amber text at bottom

narration: Seventy-five percent of people replace phones that aren't even broken. You're spending a thousand dollars for features you'll stop noticing in a week. Here's where it gets brutal.
visual: Light background with large '75%' in amber, progress bar filling to 75%, phone icon showing battery indicator still healthy with a crossed-out trash icon overlay

narration: Upgrading every two years is five hundred dollars a year leaving your pocket. That's every single year, forever. Now let's run the actual math.
visual: Dark background, '$500/YR' in large white text, animated bar chart growing across years labeled YR5, YR10, YR20, YR30, YR40 in amber bars

narration: Invest that five hundred a year at the market's average 10% return for 40 years. The number animating on screen right now is not a typo.
visual: Light background, large dollar counter animating from $0 up to $221,000 in amber, '10% MARKET RETURN • 40 YEARS' label below in dark text

narration: You don't have to swear off phones. Just stretch each upgrade from two years to four. That one move gets you more than a hundred thousand dollars back.
visual: Dark background, two comparison cards: top card '2 YEARS' with red border showing '$221K LOST', bottom card '4 YEARS' with amber border showing 'SAVE $110K'

narration: Your next upgrade decision is worth $221,000. Share this with someone who's about to buy a new phone right now — they need to see this math.
visual: Light background, golden piggy bank SVG, '$221,000' in bold amber above it, 'SHARE THIS' call to action with upward arrow, follow prompt at bottom
